I treated the prompt as fixing the special tame-format presentation and the prior log as a proposal record, not as proof. The prompt requires distinguished tame generators sigma,tau, with the relation tau^sigma=tau^2 implicit, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate after many finite-quotient failures of earlier scalar/plain-ŌÄ2/fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,with ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant in this word, since that exponent is applied only to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

1. What I regard as actually solid I agree with the late prior turns on the two algebraic conventions, but only when stated as equations.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, let V=kerőļ, let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

The safe identity is (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít,because ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) on t and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on odd tame characters. ThusDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

So Da is radical and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. This is the real reason nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks have only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

The elimination polynomial should be defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. Writing the reciprocal is dangerous because 1+T is not a unit in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With this convention,h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, since (1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1),and 2s+1 is coprime to f. This keeps the prior abelianization and sign-kernel conclusions internally consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.These are strong candidate-side checks, not a proof of the full profinite presentation.2. New contribution: explicit projectors for the next first-IA test (e,f)=(9,6)

Turn 60 identified (e,f)=(9,6) as the next structurally new first-IA arithmetic test after (5,4). I agree, and I sharpened it into a concrete projection test.

Let f=6=2n, so n=3, and take the anti-cyclotomic tame orderd=2n+1=9.The mixed first-IA obstruction module, after candidate normalization and after quotienting by the odd Hall part, isF2[C9]‚ČÉF2 [T]/(T9‚ąí1).

Since T9‚ąí1=T9+1 over F2,T9+1=(T+1)(T2+T+1)(T6+T3+1).ThusF2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.This is not just a qualitative decomposition. The three orthogonal idempotent projectors in F2 [T]/(T9+1) are:E1=1+T+T2+T3+T4+T5+T6+T7+T8,E3=T+T2+T4+T5+T7+T8,E9=T3+T6.

They satisfyE1+E3+E9=1,Ei2=Ei,EiEj=0(iÓÄ†=j),and project respectively onto the F2, F4, and primitive-9 F64 components.

So if the arithmetic-minus-candidate mixed residual is

R(T)=j=0‚ąĎ8rjTj‚ąąF2[C9],then the next test can be done componentwise:R1=E1R,R3=E3R,R9=E9R.

The candidate-normalized target isR1=R3=R9=0,together with zero in the two universal square directions b[2]and S[2].There is an even more elementary way to read these components from the coefficients r0,‚Ä¶,r8.

For the F2 -component:R1=0‚üļj=0‚ąĎ8rj=0.

For the F4 -component, group the coefficients by residue class modulo 3:A0=r0+r3+r6,A1=r1+r4+r7,A2=r2+r5+r8.ThenR3=0‚üļA0=A1=A2.

For the primitive-9 component,R9=0 is equivalent to the three period-3 equalitiesr0=r3=r6,r1=r4=r7,r2=r5=r8.Thus the primitive-9 component is exactly the obstruction to the residual being pulled back from the C3 -level. This makes (9,6) a genuinely new test: (3,6) cannot see R9, while (9,6) can.

3. Why this matters The late prior turns correctly reframed the first-IA space as a torsor of differences, not as raw coordinates of a single lift. Candidate-side residuals vanish when the affine origin is taken from the candidate‚Äôs own actual quadratic slice. So the only meaningful first-IA test is arithmetic-minus-candidate.The new projector package says that in the first genuinely new case (9,6), an arithmetic computation does not need to output a huge filtered operator matrix. It only needs to output:(ŌĶb,ŌĶS;R1,R3,R9)‚ąąF22‚äēF2‚äēF4‚äēF64,where ŌĶb,ŌĶS are the two universal square coordinates and R1,R3,R9 are the mixed anti-cyclotomic residual projections.The target is the zero tuple. A nonzero R9 would be the first primitive-9 filtered-action obstruction to the live word. A zero tuple would not prove the presentation, but it would move the comparison beyond the first-IA anti-cyclotomic layer.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I froze the two stable conventionsDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.New this turn: for the next structurally new first-IA test (e,f)=(9,6), I decomposed the mixed residual moduleF2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64 with explicit projectorsE1=j=0‚ąĎ8Tj,E3=T+T2+T4+T5+T7+T8,E9=T3+T6.This gives a concrete coefficient-level test for the primitive-9 obstruction.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is an arithmetic comparison, or a dyadic formation theorem, showing that the actual local Galois action has the same candidate-normalized affine origins over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: For L=Q2(ő∂9,ŌĖ), ŌĖ9=2, fix a Frobenius lift and a normal-basis gauge. Compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate first-IA residual(ŌĶb,ŌĶS;R(T))and project R(T) using E1,E3,E9. The target is zero in F22‚äēF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.[BREAKTHROUGH]
